must_have_breakdown.evidence: Resume explicitly lists Git under DevOps tools alongside Copado and SFDX.
Resume lists REST integration experience but entirely within the Salesforce ecosystem, not Python-based backend services.
REST/SOAP integrations imply JSON usage but no explicit Python-based JSON handling is evidenced.
OOP principles are evident via Apex framework design (Triggers, Batch, Queueable), though not in Python.
Resume references a Salesforce test framework with 90%+ coverage but no PyTest or Python unit testing.
Holds 'Salesforce Certified AI Specialist' and 'AI Associate' certifications but no evidence of hands-on AI model training or Python-based ML projects.

reasoning: Reject — wrong domain entirely. This candidate is a Senior Salesforce Developer with 7 years of Apex, LWC, and Salesforce platform experience. The role requires a Junior Python Developer building back-end services with Django/Flask/FastAPI, relational and NoSQL databases, and Docker/Jenkins tooling. There is zero Python development evidence anywhere in this resume. The recruiter specifically wants candidates who have built Python projects and trained AI models — this candidate holds Salesforce AI certifications but shows no evidence of hands-on model training or Python ML work. The only transferable signals are Git usage and general REST integration experience, both of which are deeply context-locked to the Salesforce ecosystem. Do not shortlist — this is a domain mismatch, not a level mismatch.
strengths: GIT & CI/CD DISCIPLINE: Actively uses Git, Copado, SFDX, and CI/CD branching strategy in current IBM role — demonstrates solid version control hygiene, which is one of the few transferable skills to the Python dev role.

SALESFORCE AI CERTIFICATIONS: Holds both 'Salesforce Certified AI Specialist' and 'AI Associate' credentials — a rare signal, but scoped entirely to Salesforce AI features, not Python-based model training or ML pipelines the recruiter requires.

PRODUCTION-SCALE DELIVERY TRACK RECORD: Led end-to-end Salesforce delivery for 1500+ users, reduced deployment defects by 30%, and maintained 90%+ test coverage across 500+ classes — strong engineering discipline that would translate if the domain were aligned.
gaps: ZERO PYTHON EXPERIENCE: The entire resume is Salesforce/Apex-only. There is no Python code, no Django/Flask/FastAPI project, no database work in MySQL/PostgreSQL/MongoDB — this is the single most disqualifying factor for a Python Developer role.

NO AI MODEL TRAINING EVIDENCE: The recruiter explicitly requires hands-on AI model training experience. Salesforce AI certifications do not demonstrate this — there are no Python ML projects, no model training pipelines, and no relevant technical depth in this area.

scoring_factors.reason: No notice period stated in resume and JD has no maximum notice period specified.
Candidate location is not stated in resume; cannot confirm or deny Bangalore match.
JD requires 0 years relevant experience; no experience floor penalty applicable.
JD gender preference is 'Any'; no penalty applied.
JD has no college preference or exclusion; no penalty or bonus applied.
